title: default(T) и default-литерал для параметра типа

cards.prompt: default(T) is polymorphic over T: value types give a zero (int 0, bool False), reference types give null, and int? (Nullable<int>) gives null (not 0), so the chain prints 0|False|null|null
cards.code: using System; static string D<T>() { var v = default(T); return v == null ? "null" : v.ToString(); } Console.WriteLine($"{D<int>()}|{D<bool>()}|{D<string>()}|{D<int?>()}");

cards.prompt: The default literal in an optional parameter T fill = default equals default(T); filling arrays gives 0 for int and False for bool, so the output is 0,0,0 | False,False
cards.code: using System; static T[] Init<T>(int n, T fill = default) { var a = new T[n]; for(int i=0;i<n;i++) a[i]=fill; return a; } Console.WriteLine($"{string.Join(",", Init<int>(3))} | {string.Join(",", Init<bool>(2))}");

cards.prompt: typeof(T).Name yields the CLR name (Int32, Double) via reification, and default(T) yields their zero; double's ToString prints 0 (not 0.0), so the output is Int32=0 Double=0
cards.code: using System; static string DD<T>() { var v = default(T); return $"{typeof(T).Name}={(v==null?"null":v.ToString())}"; } Console.WriteLine($"{DD<int>()} {DD<double>()}");
